content: Autonomous agentic AI’s core value of independent operation does not require eliminating working memory capacity limits; instead, intentionally bounded working memory can act as an adaptive filtering mechanism that prevents infinite task branching and keeps the agent focused on high-priority human-aligned goals. We can reframe working memory limits not as a flaw to fix, but as a designed feature that improves the real-world utility of fully autonomous agentic AI systems.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
